name: Email Received From Potentially Hostile Host
notes: This rule triggers when an email is received from a host that is known for hostile activities, such as Phishing, Spam, Malware, Botnet Command and Control, or Cryptocurrency Mining.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Originating Host
when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Mail Receive, System.Content Information
TD_list: APPLY Email Received From Potentially Hostile Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Originating Host
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Mail Receive, System.Content Information
RED_list: Event name: Email Received From Potentially Hostile Host
Event description: Detects when an email is received from a host that is known for hostile activities, such as Phishing, Spam, Malware, Botnet Command and Control, or Cryptocurrency Mining.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Sender (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
